records.ety: [OE. Trivigant, Termagant, Termagant (in sense 1), OF. Tervagan; cf. It. Trivigante.]

defs: An imaginary being supposed by the Christians to be a Moslem deity or false god. He is represented in the ancient moralities, farces, and puppet shows as extremely vociferous and tumultuous.
quotes: The lesser part on Christ believed well, On Termagant the more, and on Mahound.

defs: A boisterous, brawling, turbulent person; -- formerly applied to both sexes, now only to women.
quotes: This terrible termagant, this Nero, this Pharaoh.
The slave of an imperious and reckless termagant.

defs: Tumultuous; turbulent; boisterous; furious; quarrelsome; scolding.
quotes: A termagant, imperious, prodigal, profligate wench.
